evals.prompt: Build an operator console for a document-processing platform. The operators need to ingest documents, validate extracted fields, browse an archive of processed records, export batches, run QA on low-confidence extractions, and manage extraction-profile configuration. Stack is your choice. Save the UI scaffold to /home/z/my-project/ops-console.
evals.expected_output: A multi-page backoffice UI scaffold with one route per capability (ingest, validation, archive, export, qa, settings/extraction-profiles), a shared shell/navigation, list/detail patterns, server-side filtering and pagination for the archive, loading/empty/error/permission states, a dedicated configuration page, and an AI-assistant surface that states it does not mutate business state. Design patterns from design-patterns.md and web-quality.md applied (focus states, 44px targets, no color-only meaning, Core Web Vitals budgets, security baseline).
expectations: Multiple capability pages exist, not a single decorative dashboard
A shared shell or navigation ties the pages together
The archive/records view uses server-side filtering and pagination, not an unbounded client list
Loading, empty, error, and permission-denied states are present for at least one critical workflow
A dedicated configuration page distinguishes technical keys from user-facing labels and supports add/edit/remove
An AI-assistant surface states it will not modify business state unless explicitly required
Destructive actions (e.g. export/delete) require confirmation
Accessibility basics present: visible focus, keyboard-reachable controls, no color-only status, errors announced via role=alert/aria-live
Performance: explicit bundle/CSS budget, long lists virtualized, search/filter debounced, no CLS from late content
Security baseline: HTTPS-only, CSP/Trusted Types for HTML sinks (no innerHTML=untrusted), third-party pinned with SRI, no leaked source maps
No backend business rule (auth, lifecycle, eligibility) is duplicated client-side as authoritative

evals.prompt: Review this admin dashboard at /home/z/my-project/legacy-admin for UX problems before we ship it. Focus on operator usability and accessibility. Write findings to /home/z/my-project/admin-review.md.
evals.expected_output: A review document listing concrete UX/accessibility/performance/security defects found in the dashboard, mapped to the skill's design-pattern disciplines (accessibility, touch, performance/Core Web Vitals, layout, typography/color, animation, forms, navigation, charts, security).
expectations: Flags missing or insufficient contrast and removed focus rings if present
Flags hover-only interactions that fail keyboard/touch
Flags any unbounded client-side list without pagination or virtualization
Flags missing loading/empty/error states on data views
Flags charts that encode meaning by color alone without legend/label
Flags sub-44px touch targets and missing labels on icon-only actions
Flags performance regressions: render-blocking JS, no reserved space for images/embeds (CLS), undebounced input, missing bundle budget
Flags security gaps: mixed content, missing CSP/Trusted Types, third-party scripts without SRI, innerHTML fed untrusted input
Review is framed as patterns/anti-patterns, not stack-specific code fixes
